sources.exact_document_title: Statement on Post-Dural Puncture Headache Management
sources.version: Approved by the ASA House of Delegates on 2021-10-13
sources.population: Predominantly obstetric patients with recognised or suspected unintentional dural puncture or post-dural puncture headache.
sources.clinical_setting: Neuraxial anesthesia follow-up, postpartum assessment, discharge, and post-discharge follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documented headache history, focused examination, atypical or severe features, and follow-up after suspected or confirmed dural puncture. It is only partial for a broad non-obstetric post-spinal-headache workflow, and treatment recommendations are not mapped.

exact_sections.heading: PDPH Definition and PDPH Diagnosis
exact_sections.locator: official statement, PDPH Definition and PDPH Diagnosis paragraphs
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the dural-puncture context, headache timing and features, associated symptoms, focused history and examination, and atypical or severe neurological features without automatically diagnosing PDPH.

exact_sections.heading: Follow-up and Summary Recommendations
exact_sections.locator: official statement, Follow-up paragraphs and Summary Recommendations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documented follow-up until symptom resolution, brief neurological assessment, and verbal or written contact advice as clinician-completed actions without generating treatment.
